name: Measurable Riemann Mapping Theorem
description: The Measurable Riemann Mapping Theorem (MRMT): For a Beltrami differential μ on ℂ with ‖μ‖∞ < 1, the Beltrami equation ∂̄f = μ ∂f has a unique quasiconformal solution f: ℂ→ℂ fixing 0, 1, ∞. Equivalently: any measurable conformal structure on ℂ̂ is realized by a unique quasiconformal map. Central to Sullivan's no wandering domain proof: deform conformal structure along orbits to produce contradictions.
mermaid:
graph TD
    DefBeltrami["Def: Beltrami differential μ\nμ(z) dz̄/dz, ‖μ‖∞<1"]
    DefQC["Def: Quasiconformal map\nBounded dilatation K"]
    DefBeltramiEq["Def: Beltrami equation\n∂̄f = μ ∂f"]
    ThmMRMT["Thm: MRMT\nUnique QC solution fixing 0,1,∞"]
    ThmMeasurable["Thm: Measurable conformal structure\nRealized by unique QC map"]
    LemExistence["Lem: Existence via PDE\nElliptic regularity"]
    LemUniqueness["Lem: Uniqueness\nNormalization"]
    DefBeltrami --> DefBeltramiEq
    DefQC --> ThmMRMT
    DefBeltramiEq --> LemExistence
    DefBeltramiEq --> LemUniqueness
    LemExistence --> ThmMRMT
    LemUniqueness --> ThmMRMT
    ThmMRMT --> ThmMeasurable
